name: support-triage-v1
description: Illustrative rubric for a support ticket triage workload. These thresholds are an EXAMPLE of the format, not a recommendation - what 'good enough' means depends on what happens downstream of a misclassified ticket, which only you know. Agree your own numbers with the stakeholder who owns the outcome, and do it before the run.

dimensions.label: Label accuracy on tickets moved to the cheap model
dimensions.rationale: Only the routed-weak calls are at risk; calls kept on the strong model score 1.0 by definition and would dilute this. Below 0.90 a human has to re-check every ticket, which erases the saving.

dimensions.label: No single routed call scored zero
dimensions.rationale: A mean can look healthy while one ticket type fails completely. On a classification workload this catches a category the small model simply cannot recognize.

dimensions.label: Share of spend saved vs all-strong baseline
dimensions.rationale: Below 20% the operational cost of running a second model outweighs the saving.

dimensions.label: p95 latency of the call a user waits on
dimensions.rationale: Triage runs on a background sync, so this is generous. Tighten it hard for anything a person watches.

dimensions.label: No failed calls
dimensions.rationale: A run with failed calls has excluded them from every other number here. Fix the run before reading the verdict.
